name: Greater London (United Kingdom)
description: A blue field bearing the white Greater London Authority emblem — the curving 'London' wordmark used by the GLA, Mayor of London, and Transport for London.
history: Adopted in 2000 with the establishment of the Greater London Authority. Distinct from the centuries-old flag of the City of London (which is the St George's Cross with the sword of St Paul).

features.role: Greater London Authority emblem — 'London' wordmark with the curved 'GLA' device
features.symbolism: Greater London — the metropolitan area — distinct from the historic City of London
